pattern: A single write pointer compacts all non-zero values to the front in their original order, after which the tail is filled with zeros, moving every zero to the end in place with one pass and constant extra space.
hints: Keep the relative order of the non-zero numbers; only the zeros need to drift to the back.
Use a slot index that marks where the next non-zero value belongs, advancing it only when you place a value.

Walk the array copying each non-zero value into the slot position and incrementing the slot; then set every remaining position from slot to the end to zero (or swap as you go to avoid the second pass).

edge_inputs.description: zeros interleaved with non-zeros keep order
no zeros, array must be unchanged
negatives preserved in order around zeros

stress.description: large array seeded with many zeros to stress order preservation of the non-zero values
